title: asplenium ceterach subsp. cyprium
intro: Asplenium ceterach subsp. cyprium is a subspecies of the fern Asplenium ceterach, commonly known as the rustyback or rough buckler fern, which is a type of vascular plant native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Asplenium ceterach subsp. cyprium include the potential to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, as well as its use in treating respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems. The plant is often prepared in herbal tea or infusions, where it is steeped in hot water to release its bioactive compounds and provide its medicinal benefits.
